name: Running circular at Montgó from Denia
name_original: Running circular al Montgó desde Denia
region_intro: Jogging in El Montgó offers diverse running routes across a prominent mountain landscape. This region, situated between Dénia and Jávea, features steep elevations, craggy cliffs, and Mediterranean scrubland. The Montgó Natural Park encompasses over 2,000 hectares, providing varied terrain from stony paths to pine forests, suitable for different running preferences. Its geological composition includes compact limestones forming escarpments, offering panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. Best jogging routes in El Montgó The most popular jogging route is Running circular at Montgó from Denia, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This challenging route provides extensive views of the Dénia and Jávea coast. Another top favourite among local runners is Montgó summit loop from Parc Natural del Montgó, a difficult 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route includes a significant elevation gain of over 1,600 feet (498 m) and offers unique views from the summit. Local runners also love the Molinos de la Marina Alta — circular por la Ruta Área Recreativa - Molins (verde oscuro), a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through historical windmill areas, often completed in about 28 minutes. Jogging in El Montgó is defined by its dramatic elevation changes, coastal views, and diverse Mediterranean ecosystems. The network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore El Montgó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó offers 14 dedicated jogging routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These trails range from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, providing diverse experiences across the natural park.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for running trails in El Montgó?
A: The running trails in El Montgó are categorized into easy, moderate, and difficult. There are 2 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes. Easy runs typically cover up to 3 miles with around 330 feet of elevation gain on mostly paved surfaces. Moderate routes can extend up to 10 miles with up to 1000 feet of elevation gain on accessible paths. Difficult runs, often over 10 miles or with more than 1000 feet of elevation gain, demand very good fitness and sure-footedness, with some sections requiring alpine experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in El Montgó?
A: Yes, El Montgó features several excellent circular running routes. For a challenging experience with extensive coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872461235/running-circular-at-montgo-from-denia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Montgó from Denia&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224880489/molinos-de-la-marina-alta-circular-por-la-ruta-area-recreativa-molins-verde-oscuro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Marina Alta — circular por la Ruta Área Recreativa - Molins (verde oscuro)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through historical windmill areas. For a more strenuous loop with summit views, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14680965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó summit loop from Parc Natural del Montgó&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the running trails in El Montgó?
A: Jogging in El Montgó offers breathtaking panoramic views. From many trails, you can see the Mediterranean Sea, the Gulf of Valencia, and the coastal towns of Dénia and Jávea. On clear days, it's even possible to spot Ibiza and Mallorca. The dramatic elevation changes provide stunning visual contrasts between the mountainous terrain and the azure waters, especially from higher points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2211732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the jogging routes?
A: Yes, El Montgó is rich in historical and cultural significance. Some routes pass by ancient Iberian settlements, old farming land, and chapels. You can also find historical windmills, such as those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/203776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Jávea&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de l'Aigua&lt;/a&gt; (Water Cave) is another notable site, featuring an inscription from 238 AD left by Roman legionnaires.

Q: What natural features can I observe while running in El Montgó Natural Park?
A: El Montgó Natural Park boasts diverse ecosystems and unique geological formations. You'll encounter stony terrain, fragrant Mediterranean scrubland with herbs like thyme and rosemary, and pine forests. The park is home to over 650 plant species, including endemic ones like the Carduncellus dianius. Geologically, the mountain features craggy cliffs and distinctive rock formations, with the Jávea side often said to resemble an elephant's head and trunk.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó offers good running conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the weather can be suitable for jogging, though higher elevations might be cooler. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended during this season to avoid the peak heat.

Q: Are there any easy running trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Absolutely. For an easy and relatively flat experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1224880489/molinos-de-la-marina-alta-circular-por-la-ruta-area-recreativa-molins-verde-oscuro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de la Marina Alta — circular por la Ruta Área Recreativa - Molins (verde oscuro)&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.7-mile route through historical windmill areas is often completed in under 30 minutes and has minimal elevation gain, making it perfect for a relaxed jog or for those new to trail running.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in El Montgó?
A: The running routes in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.36 stars from over 16 reviews. Runners frequently praise the dramatic elevation changes, the stunning coastal views, and the diverse Mediterranean ecosystems. The variety of trails, catering to all ability levels, from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, is also a significant draw.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Montgó summit without a full ascent?
A: Yes, several trails offer impressive views of the Montgó summit and its dramatic profile without requiring a full, strenuous ascent. Routes on the lower slopes or those traversing the natural park provide excellent vantage points to appreciate the mountain's grandeur and its unique geological formations, such as the 'elephant's head' shape visible from Jávea.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by interesting caves in the area?
A: Yes, El Montgó is known for its caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de l'Aigua&lt;/a&gt; (Water Cave) is a notable site that can be visited. While not all running routes lead directly into caves, some trails, like the Racó del Bou – Cova de l'Aigua, offer a medium-difficulty training opportunity combined with a visit to this historic water cave. Other significant caves in the region include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while running in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó Natural Park is home to over 150 species of vertebrates. While jogging, you might spot various birds of prey, including Bonelli's eagles and peregrine falcons. Marine avifauna such as the yellow-legged gull and Audouin's gull are also present. The diverse Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests provide habitats for a variety of smaller animals, though they can be more elusive.
tips: The top of the Montgó offers unique and amazing views of the coast of Denia and Jávea. You get there along a path that in sections can be somewhat demanding due to the slopes but that is very enjoyable at every step. I recommend going around it and returning along the Camí de Colonia del Montogó, which runs along its entire length facing the sea, a wonderful walk!

Spectacular trail through the lower part of Montgó. With a good surface, a route not to get lost and to walk it at any time of the day, in winter you will have shade very early but you can still enjoy it. At night it also offers pleasant views!
